name: Fig Loans
description_short: Fig Loans offers installment loans as payday alternatives with fixed monthly payments and APRs around 190%, available in 40+ states with credit reporting to help build credit history.
description_long: Fig Loans positions itself as an alternative to traditional payday lending by offering installment loans with structured repayment terms rather than balloon payments. The company has operated since at least 2016 and has achieved B Corp certification, indicating third-party verification of social and environmental performance standards. Their core product is installment loans ranging from at least $300, with repayment periods of 4, 6, or 8 months, available across 40+ U.S. states including all featured states (Florida, Missouri, Ohio, Texas, Utah, Wisconsin) and many others. Fig Loans distinguishes itself through transparent pricing with no hidden fees, fixed monthly payment schedules similar to traditional installment loans, and credit reporting to major bureaus since 2016 to help borrowers build credit. The company emphasizes that their APR structure (exemplified at 190% for a $300 loan) is substantially lower than typical payday loans rolled over multiple times, which can exceed 391% APR. They highlight three key features: transparent terms with no fine print surprises, credit-building through bureau reporting, and repayment structures designed to be completable within the stated term. However, honest assessment reveals that while Fig positions itself as a payday alternative, APRs around 190% remain substantially higher than mainstream personal loans or credit union products, and only modestly lower than the worst payday scenarios. The company's comparison to 7-rollover payday loans is favorable but potentially misleading as it compares to a worst-case scenario rather than the typical single payday loan. Borrowers should recognize this as a higher-cost credit product better suited to emergency situations where traditional lending is unavailable.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Fixed monthly payments over 4, 6, or 8-month terms prevent debt-trap rollover cycles common in payday lending
Reports all payments to credit bureaus since 2016, enabling genuine credit-building for borrowers establishing history
Transparent APR disclosure with no hidden fees — stated rate is the actual cost
Available in 40+ states with multiple state options, providing broader geographic access than some lenders
B Corp certified, indicating third-party verification of social responsibility and accountability standards
Comparison tool on website demonstrates concrete savings vs. traditional payday loans with rollover cycles
Installment structure modeled after home loan repayment, creating predictable payoff timeline
cons: APRs around 190% remain very high compared to credit union loans, personal loans, or credit cards — 5-10x higher than mainstream alternatives
Minimum loan amount appears to be $300, which may not serve consumers needing smaller emergency amounts under $200
Website comparison uses worst-case payday scenario (7 rollovers) rather than standard payday loan, potentially overstating relative advantage
No disclosed information about approval rates, credit requirements, or actual APR ranges customers receive — site shows only examples
Limited transparency on alternative loan amounts and terms beyond the $300/4-month example provided
best_for: Borrowers with poor or no credit history who cannot qualify for traditional personal loans or credit union PALs
Individuals facing genuine emergencies who need $300-$1000+ and want to avoid payday loan debt traps with balloon payments
Credit builders seeking to establish payment history through a lender reporting to major credit bureaus
diagnosis: Fig Loans is best for borrowers with limited credit access who need $300+ in emergency funds and want to avoid payday loan rollover traps through structured installment repayment. The critical caveat is that 190% APRs, while lower than worst-case payday scenarios, remain 5-10x higher than mainstream personal loans and credit union alternatives — borrowers should exhaust traditional lending options first before considering this as a higher-cost emergency solution.
services: Installment loans with 4, 6, or 8-month repayment terms
Fixed monthly payment structure with predictable payoff dates
Loan amounts starting at $300 with options for larger loans
Credit bureau reporting to all three major bureaus for credit-building
Transparent APR disclosure with no hidden fees
State-specific loan availability (customizable by state selection)
Comparison tools showing savings vs. traditional payday loans
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
